phase-plan.purpose: Turn Grime Time's internal operations into a measurable system: quote workflow, editable quote math, internal dashboard command center, KPI scorecard, operating cadence, and phased growth guidance for tools and equipment.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: internal quote workflow, staff dashboard UX, profitability and revenue definitions, MRR framing, Payload-backed liabilities and scorecard row collections (merged with code defaults), seeded CRUD defaults for ladders/liabilities/scorecard rows, scorecard manual values powering KPI cards, scheduled-job reads on the day-board calendar, left-rail command-center detail panel (no overlay sheets), KPI cards above the command center, admin dual-nav to `/ops` and customer `/dashboard` preview, optional `PORTAL_ADMIN_ONLY` gate on portal layout, deep links from `/ops` to Payload admin for ladders and scorecard, internal quote/service-plan-derived KPI context, growth/asset ladder CRUD collections, KPI and scorecard tooltips via Internal ops targets global, admin-editable display strings via Payload global internalOpsSettings, and draftable/publishable quote settings via Payload global quoteSettings. Out of scope: customer-facing quote editing, automated tax-rate engine, finalized accounting sync, and in-portal write APIs for milestones/assets/liabilities beyond Payload admin (use admin links).

file.description: Dashboard, portal, admin, and quote UX planning.
Quote workflow and tax-process guidance.
Scorecard, asset ladder, milestones, and operating rhythm.
Customer dashboard route.
Admin-only internal command-center route.
Portal sidebar shortcuts for admin quote tools and docs.
Left-rail command-center navigator and ops detail panes.
Business momentum chart with responsive metric controls.
Portal shell overflow boundaries and sidebar scroll containment.
App-side scorecard and growth config.
Dashboard-specific display data and operator board content.
Admin-only global for ops KPI display strings and chart disclaimer.
Draftable quote math and service-toggle global used by the public instant quote flow.
Server-side reader that maps published or draft quote settings into the instant quote engine.
Calendar-based day board focused on scheduled jobs, requested jobs, and service workload visibility.
Admin-only service-appointment feed for the day-board calendar.
Admin-only growth ladder rows for `/ops` milestones tab.
Admin-only asset ladder rows for `/ops` assets tab.
Admin-only liability checklist rows for `/ops` scorecard liabilities sub-tab.
Admin-only scorecard definition rows merged with code defaults on `/ops`.
Merges static business scorecard with Payload `ops-scorecard-rows`.
Portal shell; optional `PORTAL_ADMIN_ONLY` redirect for non-admins.

task.description: Keep quote scope internal-only, surface Quotes and Quote settings directly in the admin portal, and align field and automation changes with tax-review guidance.

Make `/ops` the internal command center with KPI cards, chart, operator panel, left-rail command navigation, daily board, scorecard, milestones, asset guidance, controlled scrolling, and Payload-native operating context while `/dashboard` stays customer-facing.
Define KPI formulas, growth thresholds, and asset/tool guidance, then make sure scorecard/liability CRUD, quote pipeline math, and service-plan data actually power the live dashboard.
Shift KPI cards to compact number-first UX with tooltip-driven formula/composition disclosure, shared KPI metadata mapping, and reduced explanatory body copy in the default dashboard view.

questions.question: KPI display strings and chart disclaimer: editable via Payload global internalOpsSettings (D-ops-002). Full scorecard row definitions remain in code until a broader ops content model is needed.
Instant quote rates and service toggles live in Payload global quoteSettings with drafts enabled; the public site reads published settings while staff can preview drafts via draft mode.
Default KPI cards should be number-first and compact; explanatory detail moves to info-icon tooltips backed by shared KPI metadata for formula, source composition, and freshness notes.
Will revenue, margin, and liability data stay manual in v1 or sync from QuickBooks later?
At what revenue or route-density threshold should larger-area commercial work become a formal target?
